articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables efficient on-device function calling for tasks like calendar management and hardware control, all offline. This innovation enhances mobile AI experiences by providing high-speed, low-latency performance.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes mobile AI by enabling complex tasks offline, enhancing user experience.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
articles.snippet: Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a robust ecosystem of third-party tools and integrations. Connect your agents to GitHub, Notion, Hugging Face, and more to build capable, real-world applications.
articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports various third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This ecosystem aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to create more versatile AI applications through expanded integrations.

articles.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
articles.snippet: Google I/O 2026 is returning May 19-20 at Shoreline Amphitheatre in Mountain View, CA. But before the keynotes begin, you can get into the spirit of the event with our annual tradition: the save the date puzzle.  This year's experience highlights how AI can empower and accelerate
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This annual tradition aims to engage attendees before the main event.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights how AI is integrated into event experiences, setting the tone for innovation.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event emphasizes the transition to agentic AI and offers specialized technical tracks.
articles.why_it_matters: Provides developers with essential tools and insights for navigating the evolving AI landscape.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering faster GPU and NPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance efficiency and security in deploying GenAI and on-device inference.
articles.why_it_matters: Solidifies LiteRT as a key framework for high-performance AI applications.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. Led by Google Cloud experts, it covers advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Encourages practical skill development in cloud technologies, fostering a more capable developer community.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
articles.snippet: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced a suite of updates focused on streamlining the core coding workflow through high-velocity tools like Agent Mode with Auto Approve and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.
articles.ai_summary: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These features aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Transforms AI into a more effective coding partner, enhancing developer productivity.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.snippet: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.snippet: The viral AI agentic tool let attackers silently gain admin unauthenticated access.

articles.why_it_matters: Highlights urgent security vulnerabilities in AI technologies that could impact user safety.

articles.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
articles.snippet: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the environmental trade-offs of powering AI infrastructure.

articles.why_it_matters: Shows the growing tension between tech development and community acceptance.

articles.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
articles.snippet: OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by The Verge. Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of applications - says in the memo that she will be stepping away on medical leave "for the next several weeks" due to […]

articles.why_it_matters: Signals potential instability in leadership during critical AI development phases.

articles.title: Apple’s best product ever
articles.snippet: All week, we've been asking you to help us rank the 50 best products Apple ever made, as we mark the company's 50th anniversary. Thanks to everyone who pitched in - we ended up with more than 1.6 million votes! We also have lots of other coverage of Apple's first half century, and you should […]
articles.ai_summary: In celebration of its 50th anniversary, Apple has engaged its audience to rank its top 50 products, receiving over 1.6 million votes. This reflects the company's significant impact on technology and consumer culture.
articles.why_it_matters: Emphasizes Apple's enduring influence and legacy in tech innovation.

articles.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
articles.snippet: Utah is allowing an AI system to prescribe psychiatric drugs without a doctor. It's only the second time the state - and the country - has delegated this kind of clinical authority to AI. State officials say it could bring costs down and ease care shortages, but physicians warn the system is opaque, risky, and […]
articles.ai_summary: Utah has permitted an AI system to prescribe psychiatric medications without a doctor, marking a significant step in AI's role in healthcare. While it could reduce costs, concerns about safety and transparency persist.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises important questions about AI's role in critical healthcare decisions.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Block External Domain/User action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
articles.snippet: Block External Domain/User action in Microsoft Purview DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive, gives organizations stronger control over who can access sensitive files. articles.snippet:
Microsoft is releasing today 3 new foundational MAI models via its Azure Foundry platform, while Google is launching new Gemma 4 open models that can run locally.
The post Microsoft and Google Launch New AI Models appeared first on Thurrott.com.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft and Google both launched new AI models, with Microsoft introducing foundational MAI models via Azure and Google releasing Gemma 4 open models. This indicates a competitive landscape in AI development.
articles.why_it_matters: Marks significant advancements in AI technology from two major players, impacting developers.

articles.title: A popular novel has been pulled over AI concerns. Here’s what happened
articles.snippet: Hachette Book Group has removed Shy Girl by Mia Ballard from publication in the US and UK
articles.ai_summary: Hachette Book Group has decided to pull the novel 'Shy Girl' by Mia Ballard from publication in the US and UK due to concerns related to AI. This action highlights the growing scrutiny and ethical considerations surrounding AI's influence on creative works.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals increasing caution in the publishing industry regarding AI's impact on literature.

articles.title: Even Microsoft knows Copilot shouldn't be trusted with anything important
articles.snippet: Terms admit it is for entertainment only and may get things wrong A recent surge of interest in Microsoft's Terms of Use for Copilot is a reminder that AI helpers are really just a bit of fun.…
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's Terms of Use for Copilot emphasize that the AI tool is intended for entertainment and may not be reliable for important tasks. This acknowledgment reflects a broader awareness of the limitations and potential pitfalls of AI assistants.
articles.why_it_matters: Reinforces the need for users to critically assess AI tools before relying on them.

articles.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
articles.snippet:
Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies.
The post Cookie-contro…
articles.ai_summary: The article discusses the use of cookie-controlled PHP webshells in Linux hosting environments, highlighting how they evade detection through obfuscation and cron-based persistence. This tradecraft poses significant security risks.
articles.why_it_matters: Reveals advanced tactics in web security, crucial for protecting Linux environments.

articles.title: How 'High Agency' Became a Buzzword for Risky Living
articles.snippet: The buzzy label you're supposed to aspire to right now isn't "driven" or "ambitious"—it's "high agency." In an op-ed for the New York Times , Sophie Haigney traces how a once-obscure philosophical term about the human capacity for action has become a tech-wor…
articles.ai_summary: The term 'high agency' has gained popularity, representing a philosophical concept about taking action and responsibility. The op-ed discusses its rise in tech culture and its implications for personal development.
articles.why_it_matters: Reflects evolving cultural values in tech, emphasizing personal empowerment.

articles.title: Microsoft wants Edge to automatically open by default every time you turn on your Windows 11 PC
articles.snippet: A new update now in testing sets Edge to automatically appear at startup, and you are forced to opt-out if you don't want it.

articles.why_it_matters: Raises questions about user choice and control in software ecosystems.

articles.title: GitHub Will Use Copilot Interaction Data from Free, Pro, and Pro+ Users to Train AI Models
articles.snippet: GitHub will use Copilot interaction data from Free, Pro, and Pro+ users to train AI models starting April 24, opting in by default. Collected data includes code snippets, inputs, outputs, and navigation patterns from active sessions, including private repos. …
articles.ai_summary: GitHub plans to use interaction data from its Copilot tool across all user tiers to train AI models, with users automatically opted in. This raises privacy concerns regarding data usage.
articles.why_it_matters: Impacts user privacy and data ethics in AI training practices.
